questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Consider the circle $x^2+y^2-4 x-2 y+c=0$ whose centre is $A(2,1)$. If the point $P(10,7)$ is such that the line segment $P A$ meets the circle in $Q$ with $P Q=5$, then $c$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given equation of circle is $x^2+y^2-4 x-2 y+c=0$ whose centre is $A(2,1)$.
Now, $A P=\sqrt{(2-10)^2+(1-7)^2}$ $\begin{aligned} & =\sqrt{(-8)^2+(-6)^2}=\sqrt{64+36}=\sqrt{100} \\ & =10\end{aligned}$ $\therefore \quad A Q=A P-P Q=10-5=5$ So, $Q$ is the mid-point of AP $=\left(\frac{10+2}{2}, \frac{7+1}{2}\right)=(6,4)$ Since, $Q$ lies on a circle. $\begin{array}{rlrl} & \therefore & 6^2+4^2-4(6)-2(4)+c & =0 \\ & \Rightarrow & 36+16-24-8+c & =0 \\ & \Rightarrow & 20+c & =0 \\ \Rightarrow & c & =-20\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The intercept on the line y = x by the circle <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is <math><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi></math> . The equation of the circle with <math><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi><mi></mi></math> as a diameter is â€¦â€¦
questions.solution:
We have equation of line y = x and equation of circle x2+y2-2x=0

Now, intersecting points of given line and circle
x2+x2-2x=0
⇒2x2-2x=0
⇒2xx-1=0
⇒x=0,1
When x = 0 then y =0 when x =1 then y = 1
âˆ´ Coordinates of end points of diameter AB are (0, 0) and (1, 1)
âˆ´ Required equation of circle with diameter AB
x-0x-1+y-0y-1=0
⇒x2-2+y2-y=0
⇒x2+y2-x-y=0
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two circles of equal radius $a$ cut orthogonally. If their centres are $(2,3)$ and $[5,6]$ then radical axis of these circles passes through the point
questions.solution:
Let $S_1$ be the circle with centre $(2,3)$ and radius a.
Let $S_2$ be the circle with centre $(5,6)$ and radius a.
Then $S_1 \equiv(x-2)^2+(y-3)^2-a^2=0$
and $S_2=(x-5)^2+(y-6)^2-a^2=0$
Now, radical axis of these circle is given by $S_1-S_2=0$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& (x-2)^2+(y-3)^2-a^2-(x-5)^2-(y-6)^2+a^2=0 \\
& \Rightarrow(4-4 x+9-6 y)-(25-10 x)-(36-12 y)=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 13-4 x-6 y-25+10 x-36+12 y=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 6 x+6 y-48=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad x+y=\frac{48}{6}=8 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad x+y=8
\end{aligned}
$$
Since, the given circle cut orthogonally, therefore
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2 g_1 g_2+2 f_1 f_2=C_1+C_2 \\
& \Rightarrow 2(-2)(-5)+2(-3)(-6)=\left((-2)^2+(-3)^2-a^2\right) \\
& +\left((-5)^2+(-6)^2-a^2\right) \\
& {\left[\because \text { radius }=\sqrt{g^2+f^2-c} \quad \therefore a^2=g^2+f^2-c \Rightarrow\right.} \\
& \left.c=g^2+f^2-a^2\right] \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 20+36=\left(13-a^2\right)+\left(61-a^2\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 56=74-2 a^2 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 2 a^2=74-56=18 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad a^2=9 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad a=3[\because \text { radius can't be negative }] \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
Clearly, option (c) satisfy the Eq. (i)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The abscissa of <math> <mi>A</mi> </math> and <math> <mi>B</mi> </math> are the roots of the equation <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><msup><mrow><mi>b</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and their ordinates are the roots of the equation <math><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>p</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><msup><mrow><mi>q</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. The equation of the circle with <math> <mrow> <mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi></mrow> </math> as diameter is
questions.solution:
Let,Â AÂ â‰¡x1,Â y1Â andÂ Â BÂ â‰¡x2,Â y2

x1,Â x2Â  are roots of x2+2ax-b2=0

â‡’Â x-x1Â x-x2=x2+2ax-b2

y1,Â y2 are roots of y2+2py-q2=0

Now, equation of circle with AB as diameter is

questions.q: The locus of the centre of a circle which cuts the circle <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Â </mi><mn>20</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> orthogonally and also touches the line <math><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math> is
questions.solution:
Let the general equation of circle be

x2+y2+2gx+2fy+c=0 ....... (i)

It cuts the circle x2+y2-Â 20x+4=0 orthogonally

âˆ´2Â -10g+0Â Ã—fÂ =Â c+4Â â‡’-Â 20Â g=c+4 .......(ii)

âˆµ circle (i) touches x=2

therefore,Â  perpendicular distance from centre to the tangent to the circle=radius

â‡’-g+0-212+02=g2+f2-c

â‡’g+22=Â g2+f2-c

â‡’g2+4+4g=g2+f2-câ‡’Â 4g+4=f2-c ... (iii)

on eliminating c from (ii) and (iii) we get

-16g+4=f2+4â‡’f2Â +16g=0

Hence, locus of -g,Â -f is,

y2-Â 16x=0 (replacing -fÂ  & -g by x & y )

questions.q: The distinct points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>B</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>C</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>D</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mn>3</mn><mi>a</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> are concyclic, then

Here, the endpoints of diameter are 0,1 and 1,0

xx-1+yy-1=0

⇒x2+y2-x-y=0

Now, point D satisfies this equation

⇒a13a-5=0

⇒a=0, a=513

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the chord of contact of the point $\mathrm{P}(1,1)$ with respect to the circle $S=x^2+y^2+4 x+6 y-3=0$ meet the circle $\mathrm{S}=0$ at $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{B}$, then the area of $\triangle \mathrm{PAB}$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { } S=x^2+y^2+4 x+6 y-3=0 \\ & \Rightarrow(x+2)^2+(y+2)^2=4^2\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{OP}^2=\mathrm{AP}^2+\mathrm{OA}^2 \Rightarrow 5^2=4^2+\mathrm{AP}^2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{AP}=3 \\ & \text { Let } \angle \mathrm{AOP}=\angle \mathrm{BOP}=\theta \\ & \sin \theta=\frac{3}{5} \Rightarrow \cos \theta=\frac{4}{5} \\ & \sin 2 \theta=2 \sin \theta \cos \theta=2 \times \frac{3}{5} \times \frac{4}{5}=\frac{24}{25} \\ & \Delta \mathrm{AOB}=\frac{1}{2} \times \mathrm{OA} \times \mathrm{OB} \sin 2 \theta \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \times 4 \times 4 \times \frac{24}{25}=\frac{192}{25} \\ & \Rightarrow \Delta \mathrm{OAP}=\Delta \mathrm{AOP}-\Delta \mathrm{AOQ} \\ & =\Delta \mathrm{AOP}-\frac{\Delta \mathrm{AOB}}{2} \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \times 4 \times 3-\frac{96}{25}=6-\frac{96}{25}=\frac{54}{25} \\ & \Rightarrow \Delta \mathrm{PAB}=2 \times \Delta \mathrm{OAP}=2 \times \frac{54}{25}=\frac{108}{25}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A line is drawn through a fixed point $P(\alpha, \beta)$ to cut the circle $x^{2}+y^{2}=r^{2}$ at $A$ and $B$. Then
$P A \cdot P B$ is equal to
questions.solution:
The equation of any line through the point $P(\alpha, \beta)$ is
$$
\frac{x-\alpha}{\cos \theta}=\frac{y-\beta}{\sin \theta}=k \text { (say) }
$$
Any point on this line is
$$
(\alpha+k \cos \theta, \beta+k \sin \theta)
$$
This point lies on the given circle, if
$$
(\alpha+k \cos \theta)^{2}+(\beta+k \sin \theta)^{2}=r^{2}
$$
or $k^{2}+2 k(\alpha \cos \theta+\beta \sin \theta)$
$$
+\alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}-r^{2}=0
$$
Which being quadratic in $k$, gives two values of $k$. Let $P A=k_{1}, P B=k_{2}$, where $k_{1}, k_{2}$ are the roots of Eq. (i), then
$$
P A \cdot P B=k_{1} k_{2}=\alpha^{2}+\beta^{2}-r^{2}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If one end of the diameter is $(1,1)$ and the other end lies on the line $x+y=3$, then locus of centre of circle is
questions.solution:
Let the other end be $(t, 3-t)$. 
So, the equation of the variable circle is
$$
\begin{array}{l}
(x-1)(x-t)+(y-1)(y-3+t)=0 \\
\Rightarrow x^{2}+y^{2}-(1+t) x-(4-t) y+3=0
\end{array}
$$
$\therefore$ The centre $(\alpha, \beta)$ is given by
$$
\alpha=\frac{1+t}{2}, \beta=\frac{4-t}{2}
$$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad 2 \alpha+2 \beta=5
$$
Hence, the locus is $2 x+2 y=5$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The equation of the circle whose diameter is common chord to the circles
$x^{2}+y^{2}+2 a x+c=0$ 
and $x^{2}+y^{2}+2 b y+c=0$ is
questions.solution:
Let $S_{1} \equiv x^{2}+y^{2}+2 a x+c=0$
and $S_{2} \equiv x^{2}+y^{2}+2 b y+c=0$
Equation of common chord as a diameter of the third circle
$$
\begin{aligned}
S_{1}-S_{2} &=0 \\
a x-b y &=0 \\
y &=\frac{a x}{b}...(i)
\end{aligned}
$$
On putting the value of $y$ in Eq. (i), we get
$$
\begin{array}{r}
x^{2}+\left(\frac{a^{2} x^{2}}{b^{2}}\right)+2 a x+c=0 \\
\left(a^{2}+b^{2}\right) x^{2}+2 a b^{2} x+c b^{2}=0
\end{array}
$$
Let $x_{1}$ and $x_{2}$ be the roots of the equation.
$\therefore \quad x_{1}+x_{2}=\frac{-2 a b^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}}$ and $x_{1} x_{2}=\frac{c b^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}}$
Similarly, $\left(a^{2}+b^{2}\right) y^{2}+2 b a^{2} y+c a^{2}=0$
Let $y_{1}$ and $y_{2}$ be the roots of the equation $\therefore \quad y_{1}+y_{2}=\frac{-2 a^{2} b}{a^{2}+b^{2}}$
and $\quad y_{1} y_{2}=\frac{c a^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}}$
Now, the required equation of third circle $\left(x-x_{1}\right)\left(x-x_{2}\right)+\left(y-y_{1}\right)\left(y-y_{2}\right)=0$
$x^{2}-\left(x_{1}+x_{2}\right) x+x_{1} x_{2}+y^{2}-\left(y_{1}+y_{2}\right) y$
$+y_{1} y_{2}=0$
$x^{2}+y^{2}+\frac{2 a b^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \cdot x+\frac{2 b a^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \cdot y$
$+\frac{c b^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}}+\frac{c a^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}}=0$
$\Rightarrow x^{2}+y^{2}+\frac{2 a b^{2}}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \cdot x+\frac{2 a^{2} b}{a^{2}+b^{2}} \cdot y$
$+\frac{c\left(a^{2}+b^{2}\right)}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2}\right)}=0$
$\Rightarrow x^{2}+y^{2}+\frac{2 a b^{2}}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2}\right)} \cdot x+\frac{2 a^{2} b}{\left(a^{2}+b^{2}\right)} \cdot y+c=0$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the circles $x^{2}+y^{2}+2 x+2 k y+6=0$, $x^{2}+y^{2}+2 k y+k=0$ intersect orthogonally, then $k$ is
questions.solution:
Since, circles cut orthogonally, if
$$
\begin{aligned}
& & 2 g g^{\prime}+2 f f^{\prime}=c+c^{\prime} \\
\Rightarrow & & \quad 0+2 k^{2}=6+k \\
\Rightarrow & & 2 k^{2}-k-6=0 \\
\Rightarrow & &(2 k+3)(k-2)=0
\end{aligned}
$$
$\Rightarrow \quad k=2$ or $-3 / 2$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">Q</mi></math> be the common chord of the circles <math><msub><mrow><mi>S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>:</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi>S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>:</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then the perimeter (in units) of the triangle <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">Q</mi></math> is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>where</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math>
questions.solution:
Equation of the common chord is 2x+1=0

C1M=-2+12=12
PM=94-14=2
Length of the common chord =22
Hence, the perimeter of ΔC1PQ=32+32+22
=3+22 units
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> is a point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> in the first quadrant. If the two circles which pass through <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> and touch both the coordinate axes, cut at right angles, then :
questions.solution:
Let the equation of circle which touches both coordinate axes is 

x-r2+y-r2=r2

⇒x2+y2-2rx-2ry+r2=0

where, r is radius.

It is passing through a,b, so

r2-2(a+b)r+a2+b2=0

Let the roots of this equation are r1,r2

Sum of roots

r1+r2=2a+b

Product of roots

r1·r2=a2+b2

Both circles intersect orthogonally each other so

2r1r2+2r1r2=r12+r22

⇒6r1r2=r1+r22

⇒6a2+b2=4(a+b)2

⇒a2-4ab+b2=0
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the circles <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>10</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>16</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>89</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mrow><mi>r</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>14</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>42</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> have common points, then the number of possible integral values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Centre & radius of x2+y2-10x+16y+89-r2=0 are 5,-8 & r respectively

Centre & radius of x2+y2+6x-14y+42=0 are -3,7 & 4 respectively

Distance between the centres of circles is 82+152=17

â‡’17-4â‰¤râ‰¤17+4

â‡’13â‰¤râ‰¤21

â‡’râˆˆÂ± 21,Â±20,Â±19,Â±18,Â±17,Â±16,Â±15,Â±14,Â±13

Hence, the number of possible values of r is equal to 18
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The centre of circle inscribed in square formed by the lines <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>12</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>14</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>45</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, is
questions.solution:
Given, circle is inscribed in square formed by the lines

x2-8x+12=0 and  y2-14y+45=0   

⇒x=6 & x=2 and y=5 & y=9

These lines can be plotted as

 
Here ABCD is a square. Hence, the centre of circle inscribed in this square is the point of intersection of diagonals, which is the mid-point of AC or BD
 
=2+62,5+92=4,7
 
Centre of inscribed circle is 4,7

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let the circle $\mathrm{S}$ which is concentric with the circle $x^2+y^2-2 x+k y+4=0$ pass through the point $(3,-2)$. If one of the diameters of S lies along the line $3 x-2 y+$ $4=0$, then the radius of the circle $S$ is
questions.solution:
The given equation of circle is
$x^2+y^2-2 x+k y+4=0$  ...(i)
Centre of above circle (i) is
$c \equiv\left(1,-\frac{k}{2}\right)$
$\because$ Circle $S$ is concentric with circle (i)
$\therefore \quad$ Equation of $S$ is
$(x-1)^2+\left(y+\frac{k}{2}\right)^2=r^2$   ...(ii)
The centre $\left(1, \frac{-k}{2}\right)$ lies on the line $3 x-2 y+4=0$
$\therefore \quad 3 \times 1-2 \times\left(\frac{-k}{2}\right)+4=0 \Rightarrow k=-7$
Putting the value of $k$ in eqn. (ii), we get
$(x-1)^2+\left(y-\frac{7}{2}\right)^2=r^2$    ...(iii)
$\because$ Eqn. (iii) passes through the point $(3,-2)$. Then, we have
$\begin{aligned} & (3-1)^2+\left(-2-\frac{7}{2}\right)^2=r^2 \\ & \Rightarrow r^2=4+\frac{121}{4}=\frac{137}{4} \\ & \Rightarrow r=\frac{\sqrt{137}}{2}\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Radius of the circle $S$ is, $r=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{137}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The radius of the larger circle lying in the first quadrant and touching the line $4 x+3 y-12=0$ and the co-ordinate axes, is
questions.solution:
Let the equation of the circle is
$$
x^2+y^2+2 g x+2 f y+c=0
$$
This circle touch the coordinate axes and lying in the first quadrant, then
$$
\begin{aligned}
g^2-c & =0 \text { and } f^2-c=0 \\
g & = \pm \sqrt{c}, f= \pm \sqrt{c}
\end{aligned}
$$
circle lies in first quadrant
$\therefore$ The centre is $(\sqrt{c}, \sqrt{c})$
If the line $4 x+3 y-12=0$ touch the circle, then
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \sqrt{f^2+g^2-c}=\frac{4 \sqrt{c}+3 \sqrt{c}-12}{\sqrt{4^2+3^2}} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \sqrt{c+c-c}=\frac{7 \sqrt{c}-12}{5} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 5 \sqrt{c}=7 \sqrt{c}-12 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 2 \sqrt{c}=12 \Rightarrow c=36 \\
& \text { Radius }=\sqrt{f^2+g^2-c}=\sqrt{c+c-c}=\sqrt{c}=6
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $P$ is a point such that the ratio of the square of the lengths of the tangents from $P$ to the circles $x^2+y^2+2 x-4 y-20=0$ and $x^2+y^2-4 x+2 y-2 y-44=0$ is $2: 3$, then the locus of $P$ is a circle with centre :
questions.solution:
Let co-ordinates of $P$ be $\left(x_1, y_1\right)$.
Given that,
$x^2+y^2+2 x-4 y-20=0$ ...(i)
and $\quad x^2+y^2-4 x+2 y-44=0$ ...(ii)
Length of the tangent from $P$ to Eq. (i)
$=x_1^2+y_1^2+2 x_1-4 y_1-20$ ...(iii)
Length of the tangent from $P$ to Eq. (ii)
$=x_1^2+y_1^2-4 x_1+2 y_1-44$ ...(iv)
Given that ratio of lengths of tangent $=\frac{2}{3}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{x_1^2+y_1^2+2 x_1-4 y_1-20}{x_1^2+y_1^2-4 x_1+2 y_1-44}=\frac{2}{3}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 3 x_1^2+3 y_1^2+6 x_1-12 y_1-60$ $=2 x_1^2+2 y_1^2-8 x_1+4 y_1-88$
$\Rightarrow \quad x_1^2+y_1^2+14 x_1-16 y_1+28=0$
$\therefore$ Locus of points $P$ is
$x^2+y^2+14 x-16 y+28=0$
Centre of the circle is $(-7,8)$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Consider the following statements. I. The intercept made by the circle $x^2+y^2-2 x-4 y+1=0$ on $Y$-axis is $2 \sqrt{3}$ II. The intercept made by the circle $x^2+y^2-4 x-2 y+6=0$ on $X$-axis is $2 \sqrt{2}$ III. The straight line $y=2 x+1$ cuts the circle $x^2+y^2=9$ at two distinct points Then which one of the following options is correct?
questions.solution:
(I) $x^2+y^2-2 x-4 y+1=0$ intercept on $Y$-axis is $2 \sqrt{f^2-c} \Rightarrow 2 \sqrt{4-1} \Rightarrow 2 \sqrt{3}$ True (II) $x^2+y^2-4 x-2 y-6=0$ intercept on $X$-axis $2 \sqrt{4+6} \Rightarrow 2 \sqrt{10} \neq 2 \sqrt{2}$ False
(III) Line $y=2 x+1$ at the circle $x^2+y^2-9=0$
So, $r^2-p^2>0$
$\Rightarrow \quad r^2=9 \Rightarrow p=\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}$
$\Rightarrow \quad p^2=\frac{1}{5} \Rightarrow 9-\frac{1}{5}>0$ is it is True.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The line $y=m x+c$ intercepts the circle $x^2+y^2=r^2$ in two distinct points, if
questions.solution:
Equation of the circle is
$$
x^2+y^2=r^2
$$
and the line is $m x-y+c=0$
The line (ii) intersect (i) in two distinct points, if the length of the $\perp^r$ from the centre $(0,0)$ to the line (ii) is less then $r$
Thus, $\left|\frac{0-0+c}{\sqrt{m^2+1}}\right| < r$
$$
\begin{array}{ccc}
\Rightarrow & |c| < r \sqrt{m^2+1} \\
\Rightarrow & -r \sqrt{m^2+1} < c < r \sqrt{m^2+1}
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: From a point <math><mi>P</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>3,3</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> on the circle <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>18</mn></math> two chords <math><mi>P</mi><mi>Q</mi></math> and <math><mi>P</mi><mi>R</mi></math> each of length <math><mn>2</mn></math> units are drawn on this circle. Then, the value of the length <math><mi>P</mi><mi>M</mi></math> is equal to (where, <math><mi>M</mi></math> is the midpoint of the line segment joining <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math>)
questions.solution:
QM2=QP2–PM2=OQ2-OM2
⇒4-x2=18-32-x2 (where, PM=x)
⇒62x=4⇒x=23 units
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A circle inscribed into a rhombus ABCD with one angle 60<sup>o</sup>. The distance from the centre of the circle to the nearest vertex is equal to 1. If P is any point of the circle, then <math> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mfenced close="|" open="|" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>PA</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> <mn>2</mn> </msup> <mo>+</mo> <msup> <mrow> <mfenced close="|" open="|" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>PB</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> <mn>2</mn> </msup> <mo>+</mo> <msup> <mrow> <mfenced close="|" open="|" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>PC</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> <mn>2</mn> </msup> <mo>+</mo> <msup> <mrow> <mfenced close="|" open="|" separators=","> <mrow> <mtext>PD</mtext> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> <mn>2</mn> </msup> </mrow> </math> is equal to :
questions.solution:
In ∆ODA

tan30=1x

x=3

∴ D=(3,0)

r = 3 sin 3 0 ∘ = 3 2

         

PA 2 + PB 2 + PC 2 + PD 2

= x - 3 2 + y 2 + x 2 + y - 1 2 + x + 3 2 + y 2 + x 2 + y + 1 2

=4x2+y2+2 =4 x2+2          ∵ x2+y2=r2

= 4 3 4 + 2 = 1 1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the circles described on the line joining the points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> as diameter cuts the axis of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> in points whose abscissae are the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi> </mi><mn>5</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo mathvariant="italic">,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo></math>
questions.solution:
Equation of circle in diameter





x- 0x-α+ y- 1y-β=0

on x axis y=0

x2-αx +β=0 ...1 

x2- 5x+3=0 ...2 

Compare equation 1 & 2

α=5, β=3
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following equations gives a circle?
questions.solution:
Now, taking option (a)
i.e. $r=2 \sin \theta$
Let $x=r \sin \theta, y=r \cos \theta$
$\Rightarrow \quad r^2=2 r \sin \theta$
$\Rightarrow \quad x^2+y^2=2 y \quad\left[\because x^2+y^2=r^2\right]$
This equation represents a circle.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> lies completely inside the circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then
questions.solution:
We have,

The circle x2+y2+2a1x+c=0, with centre C1-a1,0

and circle x2+y2+2a2x+c=0, with centre C2-a2,0

Equation of radical axis of the given circles is x=0. If one circle lies completely inside the other, then the centre of both circles should lie on the same side of radical axis and radical axis should not intersect the circles.

⇒a1a2>0

Putting x=0 (equation of radical axis) to anyone of the circle, we get,

 y2+c=0.

For imaginary roots, c>0 (radical axis should not intersect the circles).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The equation of the circle circumscribing the triangle formed by the lines <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>5</mn></math> is
questions.solution:
Let the triangle thus formed is △ABC, where

AB: x+y=6,BC: 2x+y=4 

and CA: x+2y=5

Now we can find coordinates of point A,B and C by solving these three eqautions.
Solving AB and BC,
we get B-2,8.
Similarly we get, 

A7,-1 and C1,2.

Now let the equation of circle passing through vertices of △ABC is x2+y2+2gx+2fy+c=0

Now, point A, B and C will satisfy this general equation. 

x2+y2+2gx+2fy+c=0

⇒72+-12+2g7+2f-1+c=0 …A

⇒-22+82+2g-2+2f8+c=0 ⋯ B

⇒12+22+2g1+2f2+c=0 …C

Solving these three equations for g,f and c we get,

⇒50+14g-2f+c=0…A

⇒68-4g+16f+c=0…B

⇒5+2g+4f+c=0 …C

⇒18-18g+18f=0=2-2g+2f=0

⇒63-6g+12f=0=21-2g+4f=0

⇒f=-192

⇒g=-172

⇒c=50

Hence equation will be,

x2+y2-17x-19y+50=0

Hence, Option 4 is the answer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $P$ is a point such that the ratio of the square of the lengths of the tangents from $P$ to the circles $x^2+y^2+2 x-4 y-20=0$ and $x^2+y^2-4 x+2 y-2 y-44=0$ is $2: 3$, then the locus of $P$ is a circle with centre :
questions.solution:
Let co-ordinates of $P$ be $\left(x_1, y_1\right)$.
Given that,
$x^2+y^2+2 x-4 y-20=0$ ...(i)
and $\quad x^2+y^2-4 x+2 y-44=0$ ...(ii)
Length of the tangent from $P$ to Eq. (i)
$=x_1^2+y_1^2+2 x_1-4 y_1-20$ ...(iii)
Length of the tangent from $P$ to Eq. (ii)
$=x_1^2+y_1^2-4 x_1+2 y_1-44$ ...(iv)
Given that ratio of lengths of tangent $=\frac{2}{3}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{x_1^2+y_1^2+2 x_1-4 y_1-20}{x_1^2+y_1^2-4 x_1+2 y_1-44}=\frac{2}{3}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 3 x_1^2+3 y_1^2+6 x_1-12 y_1-60$ $=2 x_1^2+2 y_1^2-8 x_1+4 y_1-88$
$\Rightarrow \quad x_1^2+y_1^2+14 x_1-16 y_1+28=0$
$\therefore$ Locus of points $P$ is
$x^2+y^2+14 x-16 y+28=0$
Centre of the circle is $(-7,8)$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two sides of a square are along the lines $x=-5$ and $y=4$. The point of intersection of the diagonals is $(3,-4)$. The point of intersection of the tangents drawn to the circumcircle of the square at the two consecutive vertices lying on $x=-5$ is
questions.solution: For point $\mathrm{R}(\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y})$ $$ \begin{aligned} & \left(\frac{\mathrm{x}-5}{2}, \frac{\mathrm{y}+4}{2}\right)=(3,-4) \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{R}(11,-12) \end{aligned} $$ Since PQRS is square $\therefore \mathrm{Q}(-5,-12)$ and $\mathrm{S}(4,11)$ Also PMQC is square $$ \therefore \mathrm{N}(\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y})=\left(\frac{-5-5}{2}, \frac{4,-12}{2}\right)=(-5,-4) $$ For point $\mathrm{M}(\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y})$ $$ \begin{aligned} & \left(\frac{x+3}{2}, \frac{y-4}{2}\right)=(-5,-4) \\ & \Rightarrow M(-13,-4) \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
